headline: Cyril Imhof returns repeatedly to AI agents as the next enterprise shift, arguing they are powerful, hard to build, and slow to adopt.
body: Imhof frames agentic AI as the successor to basic RAG chatbots, citing Claude handling file management, spreadsheet creation, and communications [12], while arguing adoption lags because the technology is still early rather than unpromising [2]. He stresses that building agents is harder than expected because the iterative, data-driven workflow demands a different engineering mindset [10], and he ties enterprise survival to shifting toward AI-native models [6]. On infrastructure he argues against building proprietary inference due to cost and risk [7], yet elsewhere favors local inference for privacy and control over cloud [5], alongside notes on European cloud sovereignty [9] and HBM growth at Micron and SK Hynix [1]. Separately, Caleb Writes Code gives a visual walkthrough of GPT internals: self-attention, multi-head attention, feed forward networks, positional embeddings, layer normalization, and residual connections [8].
